subject: Statistics
title: Regularization: Lasso and Ridge
description: A micro-lesson that makes Regularization: Lasso and Ridge usable.

hook.text: Your model has 500 predictors. Most are probably noise. How do you keep only the useful ones?

buildup.text: Regularization adds a penalty for large coefficients. It shrinks them toward zero, reducing overfitting.

discovery.text: Ridge regression shrinks all coefficients evenly. Lasso shrinks some to exactly zero — automatic variable selection.

twist.text: The penalty strength is a tuning parameter. Too much regularization kills real signal. Too little allows noise.

climax.text: In high-dimensional data — genomics, text, images — regularization is essential. Without it, models collapse.

punchline.text: Penalize complexity. The model keeps what matters.

quiz.question: What does lasso regularization do that ridge does not?
options: It can shrink some coefficients to exactly zero, performing automatic variable selection
It always produces more accurate predictions
It works without any tuning parameters
